title: 20 law grants at P250k, 5 MBA at P500k
primary_topics: Master's in International Business Law (MIBL) at AIM
sub_topics: increased grant amounts (P250K law, P500K Esmel)
First Lady Louise Araneta-Marcos as special guest
Fernando Zobel de Ayala as guest speaker
Kyle Tutor (1st place 2024 bar) and Love Quebrar (6th place)
Sean James Borja (1st place 2018 bar, first FLP topnotcher)
AIM Master's in International Business Law curriculum
Jikeong Kang / Olivier Roche / Felipe Calderon
FLP board composition update (Tetangco now vice chair)

stances.claim: Since its launch, FLP's scholarship program 'has always produced Bar toppers' — Sean Borja (1st place 2018 inaugural year), Kyle Tutor (1st place 2024), Love Quebrar (6th place 2024) — track-record evidence of merit-based design.

The best way to nurture prosperity is to help our people help themselves through private entrepreneurship — and entrepreneurs should be armed not just with usual business subjects but also with law backgrounders on obligations and contracts, patents, trademarks, data privacy, property rights, and statutory construction.
stances.rhetorical_move: policy thesis + enumerative law-school subject prescription for business education

stances.claim: Knowledge of the law rounds out business thinking — the law tells not only what one cannot do but how far one can go; a businessperson armed with legal knowledge 'can act with confidence and sail forward without fear.'
stances.rhetorical_move: aphoristic framing of law's enabling (not just restrictive) function

stances.claim: FLP has worked with Dr. Jikeong Kang (AIM president and dean) and her team of full-time faculty (Olivier Roche, Felipe Calderon) for eight months to produce a new Master's in International Business Law curriculum — all three AIM Esmel fellows this year are taking the MIBL.

signature_phrases: the best way to nurture prosperity is to help our people help themselves through private entrepreneurship
Knowledge of the law rounds out business thinking
Not only does the law tell what one cannot do, but it tells how far one can go
act with confidence and sail forward without fear
register_markers: LIKEWISE, IN PARTNERSHIP WITH THE METRO PACIFIC
IN PARTNERSHIP WITH THE AYALA CORP.
operational reporting tone with educational-curriculum policy insertion
decision_framework_signals: applies law-rounds-out-business framework to argue for integrated business-law-technology education
frames law as enabling (showing how far one can go) rather than purely restrictive
uses track-record evidence (consistent bar topnotchers since 2018) to validate philanthropic-program design
applies institutional-collaboration design (FLP + AIM) for curriculum innovation
notable_anecdotes: Working with Dr. Jikeong Kang, Prof. Olivier Roche, and Prof. Felipe Calderon at AIM for eight months to develop the Master's in International Business Law curriculum — all three AIM Esmel fellows this year are taking MIBL
